content.assessment: From an implementation-planning lens, the status-quo counterfactual requires no new delivery capacity because it simply continues existing administrative practice, but this is precisely its weakness: it perpetuates a mechanism with no institutionalized feedback loop, no consultation architecture, and outcomes contingent on informal political intervention rather than rule-based sequencing. Centralized closure authority (Klebelsberg Center) has historically produced repeated top-down swings without a standing demographic-trigger rule, and post-2010 curtailment of consultation mechanisms means affected communities lack a formal channel to contest or shape decisions.
opportunities: Could serve as a baseline against which any reform's added implementation costs and benefits are measured
strengths: Requires no new statutory or administrative capacity to launch, since it continues the existing centralized decision pathway (F-live-implementation_planning-01)
Politically low-risk in the short term because informal reversal after protest is an already-functioning (if unreliable) release valve (F-live-implementation_planning-02)
threats: Uneven, non-rule-based outcomes are likely to persist indefinitely absent any monitoring or adaptation capacity, since no mechanism forces evaluation of whether closures achieve stated goals (F-live-implementation_planning-02, F-live-implementation_planning-03)
Spatial unevenness in depopulation (North-East, South-West, West) will not be addressed by ad hoc, non-differentiated administrative practice (F-live-implementation_planning-04)
weaknesses: No institutionalized feedback or demographic-trigger mechanism exists; historical cycles show closures/expansions driven by top-down political decisions rather than standing rules (F-live-implementation_planning-02)

No formal consultation or appeals mechanism was in place even when risks were flagged by directors and county associations in 2016, and this gap persists post-2010 centralization (F-live-implementation_planning-03, F-live-implementation_planning-10)

Centralization of authority in a single state actor constrains any locally-sequenced adaptation to uneven regional depopulation patterns (F-live-implementation_planning-01, F-live-implementation_planning-04)
